ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Wenshan Baozhong tea; closest parent is oolong tea (beverage) FOODON_03301103.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Tea, oolong) is the closest defensible parent; no specific Baozhong sub-code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 (Oolong tea) is the closest parent; Wenshan Baozhong is a lightly oxidised sub-variety not separately coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 240 ml (one cup) of brewed Wenshan Baozhong tea, prepared by steeping tea leaves in hot water.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Wenshan Baozhong is a lightly oxidised oolong-style tea (8–15% oxidation). No dedicated USDA or HPB entry exists for this specific cultivar. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed oolong tea (NDB 14214) and brewed green tea (NDB 14355), weighted toward oolong given the tea's classification. Brewed plain tea without added sugar or milk is essentially water with trace polyphenols; macros are effectively zero. Caffeine content (~20–30 mg/100 ml) is noted but not a scored macro.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates (0.2 g/100 ml). No GI value is published for Wenshan Baozhong tea specifically. By analogy with all plain brewed teas (green, black, oolong), which contain no glycemic carbohydrates, GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel per Veritas convention for essentially zero-GI beverages). Glycemic load per 240 ml serving = 0.0. gi_category is low. This is consistent with the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables, which do not assign a GI to plain tea due to absence of glycemic carbohydrates.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (3 mg/100 ml); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Plain Wenshan Baozhong tea is appropriate for individuals with hypertension; its sodium content is negligible at approximately 3 mg per 100 ml, and regular consumption of unsweetened tea has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Effectively zero glycemic carbohydrates; no added sugar in plain preparation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain Wenshan Baozhong tea has a glycemic load of zero per serving and contains no added sugar, making it a suitable beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, commercially served versions (e.g., from bubble tea or café outlets) may contain added sugar or sweetened syrups that would significantly raise glycemic impact and should be avoided or ordered unsweetened.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; contains tea polyphenols (catechins, flavonoids) with potential cardioprotective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain Wenshan Baozhong tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides tea polyphenols that have been associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation in observational studies; it is a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed without added sugar or full-fat milk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wenshan Baozhong Tea (文山包種茶, Wénshān Bāozhǒng Chá) is a lightly oxidised oolong tea originating from the Wenshan district of New Taipei City, Taiwan. It is one of the most delicately processed teas in the oolong family, with an oxidation level of approximately 8–15%, placing it closer to green tea than to heavily oxidised oolongs. The tea leaves are traditionally wrapped in paper during processing — a practice reflected in the name 'baozhong' (包種), meaning 'wrapped kind'. The brewed liquor is pale yellow-green with a floral, fresh, and lightly grassy aroma, often described as reminiscent of orchid or gardenia blossoms. The taste is smooth, mellow, and subtly sweet with minimal astringency. In Singapore, Wenshan Baozhong tea is served at specialty Chinese tea houses and café-style outlets such as Happy Cup, typically as a hot or cold brewed tea at a premium price point. Nutritionally, plain brewed Wenshan Baozhong tea is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, negligible protein and fat, and only trace carbohydrates (0.2 g/100 ml). It provides small amounts of potassium (~20 mg/100 ml) and contains tea polyphenols including catechins and flavonoids. Caffeine content is moderate at approximately 20–30 mg per 100 ml. The glycemic index is effectively zero (set to 1 by convention), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, provided no sugar or sweetened syrups are added. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and halal.
common_user_queries: Is Wenshan Baozhong tea the same as oolong tea?

Can I drink Wenshan Bao Tea if I have high blood pressure?
Where is Wenshan Baozhong tea from?

confidence_reason: No dedicated nutrient entry exists for Wenshan Baozhong tea in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed oolong tea data, which is the closest approved-source analogue. GI is estimated by analogy with plain brewed teas per the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match was found in any primary approved source.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero: plain brewed tea contains no measurable protein per USDA SR Legacy oolong tea data. 2. fat_g is naturally zero: plain brewed tea contains no fat by nature. 3. sugar_g is confirmed zero for plain unsweetened preparation; commercially served versions at Happy Cup (Funan) may contain added sugar — nutritional values here reflect plain brewed tea only. 4. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero in brewed tea (fibre remains in spent leaves). 5. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for essentially zero-GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0 as confirmed zero or naturally zero in plain brewed tea per USDA SR Legacy data. 7. The field survey price of SGD 7.40 at Happy Cup - Funan reflects a premium specialty tea positioning. 8. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type throughout; no exact ontology code exists for Wenshan Baozhong tea specifically in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT.
